rows.imp_rat: Lingering doubt about whether an intervention breached sterility distracts the operator and erodes confidence through the rest of the run.
rows.sat_rat: With no immediate feedback, doubt about a specific intervention persists until far-downstream test results, giving little in-run reassurance.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of cycles aborted because the zone was not correctly configured before start
rows.imp_rat: An abort from wrong configuration costs the technician a full cycle and restart, so correct setup before start is a meaningful concern.
rows.sat_rat: Door interlocks and pre-cycle checklists catch many misconfigurations, so outright aborts are relatively infrequent but not eliminated.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time required to set the enclosed zone into a ready state for the decontamination cycle
rows.imp_rat: Setting the enclosed zone to a ready state is the technician's own prep step, and its duration adds directly to total unavailability.
rows.sat_rat: Standardized setup procedures make readiness fairly quick, though manual loading and sealing checks still take non-trivial hands-on time.

rows.source_job: feel reassured the handover is safe
rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of anxiety about exposing downstream personnel to residual agent at the moment of handover
rows.imp_rat: Anxiety about exposing downstream staff to residual agent weighs on the technician who personally authorizes handover.
rows.sat_rat: Trusted ppm readouts and aeration endpoints give reasonable reassurance, so the technician's exposure worry is usually contained but not absent.

rows.source_job: feel reassured the handover is safe
rows.stmt: Reduce the likelihood of feeling unsure that residual levels are truly safe when releasing the zone
rows.imp_rat: Feeling unsure that residual levels are safe at release directly affects the technician's confidence in signing off handover.
rows.sat_rat: Real-time residual sensors give the technician a fairly solid basis for confidence, tempering unease at the release point.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a release decision being overturned that erodes operations' confidence in my cleared zones
rows.imp_rat: An overturned release decision erodes operations' trust in the technician's cleared zones, affecting their perceived reliability.
rows.sat_rat: Documented, validated cycles rarely get overturned, so this is mostly well handled though a lingering risk remains.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time between an aseptic intervention occurring and its capture in the batch record during a run
rows.imp_rat: Delay between an intervention and its record entry risks inaccurate contemporaneous documentation of the operator's aseptic actions.
rows.sat_rat: Operators log interventions during the run, but glove constraints force short delays that batch-record systems only partly mitigate.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of record corrections needed to reconcile documented interventions after a run
rows.imp_rat: Post-run record corrections signal contemporaneous documentation failures that create data-integrity findings against the operator's batch record.
rows.sat_rat: Manual recording under gloves inevitably requires some reconciliation, though review-by-exception workflows reduce the volume.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of incidents that cause peers to question whether the operator can be trusted with sterility
rows.imp_rat: Repeated incidents that make peers doubt the operator's reliability threaten their professional reputation in the sterile suite.
rows.sat_rat: Trust-eroding incidents are relatively infrequent under validated isolator operations, but each media-fill or excursion failure is visible to peers.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of variation in batch throughput timing across shifts in the processing zone
rows.imp_rat: Predictable throughput timing helps the supervisor coordinate downstream flow but is a secondary concern to compliance and sterility.
rows.sat_rat: Scheduling systems provide timing visibility yet actual throughput still drifts shift to shift with staffing and disruptions.

rows.stmt: Minimize the percentage of available processing capacity left idle across shifts in the zone
rows.imp_rat: Idle capacity is a real efficiency concern the supervisor is measured on but is secondary to sterility and compliance in this zone.
rows.sat_rat: Utilization tracking exists but isolator changeover, cleaning, and decontamination cycles keep meaningful idle time baked into current operations.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of variation in batch output per shift across the available zone capacity
rows.imp_rat: Consistent batch output per shift matters for planning, but it is a productivity metric subordinate to separation assurance for this supervisor.
rows.sat_rat: Shift reporting shows output variance but current tools do little to actively level it across zone capacity.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of uncertainty that a breach might be going undetected right now
rows.imp_rat: Uncertainty that a breach could be occurring right now keeps the operator anxious and second-guessing the barrier state.
rows.sat_rat: Continuous monitoring offers partial reassurance, but the gap between checkpoints leaves genuine present-moment uncertainty unresolved.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of repeated preparation cycles caused by a failed readiness check
rows.imp_rat: Repeated preparation cycles from failed readiness checks waste hours and delay the sterile run the operator must start.
rows.sat_rat: Readiness checks usually pass, but a failed decon or leak test forces a full re-cycle with limited quick-recovery options.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of variation in cycle turnaround time from one decontamination run to the next
rows.imp_rat: Run-to-run variation complicates scheduling but matters less to the technician than achieving a valid, safe cycle each time.
rows.sat_rat: Recipe-driven cycles are fairly repeatable, yet load, ambient and aeration differences still introduce run-to-run spread the technician absorbs.

rows.source_job: feel confident the zone is truly clean
rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of second-guessing the cleanliness result because past confirmations proved unreliable
rows.imp_rat: Repeated second-guessing from unreliable past confirmations distracts the technician, though it is a softer concern than the safety outcomes.
rows.sat_rat: When indicators have proven trustworthy the technician trusts them, but occasional false reads keep some second-guessing alive.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time required to obtain formal capital authorization sign-off from the approval committee
rows.imp_rat: Speed of committee sign-off affects project schedule but the director's core stake is decision soundness rather than approval velocity.
rows.sat_rat: Established capital-approval cadences make sign-off timing fairly predictable when the case is complete.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time the operator waits for confirmation that separation is holding during a run
rows.imp_rat: Waiting for confirmation that separation is holding is a comfort factor for the operator but secondary to the physical result itself.
rows.sat_rat: Live pressure and particle displays give some ongoing reassurance, but definitive separation confirmation is not instantaneous.
